economic_notes: Paul First Nation has extensive business interests including Alberta Groups GP Ltd., Paul Band Land Holdings Ltd., Paul First Nation Cascade (GP) Inc., Paul First Nation Industry Relations Corporation, Paul First Nation Renewable Energy (GP) Inc., PFN Development Corporation, PFN Land Holdings (GP) Inc., Weh-Hnim-Nay Energy (GP) Inc., Wihneme Industrial Closure Services (GP) Corp., 399250 Alberta Ltd., PFN Business Centre (GP) Inc., PFN Construction (GP) Inc., PFN Fleet (GP) Inc., PFN Business Centre Limited Partnership, and Paul Band Group of Limited Partnerships. Ironhead Golf Course operated by Wapta Mnodai Developments Ltd. Band-owned enterprises posted net loss of $8,295,026 in 2024 vs net income of $564,522 in 2023.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED on two bases: (1) asset retirement obligations not estimated/recorded, and (2) insufficient evidence on carrying amount of investments in related First Nation entities. Band-owned enterprises recorded a net loss of $8,295,026 in 2024 (vs net income of $564,522 in 2023), significantly impacting consolidated results. Paul Band Group of Limited Partnerships had accumulated deficit of $(5,051,789). Capital lease obligations of $846,836 newly established in 2024. Deferred revenue decreased from $31.1M to $28.6M. Accumulated surplus declined from $71.9M to $63.4M. OSR calculation is negative because net income from enterprises was negative $8,295,026; total government transfers estimated as ISC $31,237,005 + CMHC $233,041 + FNDF $772,835 + ISETS $1,071,484 = $33,314,365. 15 entities accounted for by modified equity method; 2 entities (Wapta Mnodai Developments Ltd. and Paul Band Business Trust) fully consolidated.
